questions.q: Within the Trimurti, what role is Vishnu chiefly said to fulfil?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is the preserver of the Trimurti, sustaining cosmic order while Brahma creates and Shiva dissolves.

questions.q: Which goddess is described as the consort of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, the goddess of fortune and abundance, is the consort of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which bird serves as the vahana (mount) of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's vahana is the mighty eagle Garuda, sworn foe of the serpents.

questions.q: Upon which great serpent is Vishnu said to recline on the cosmic ocean?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines upon the thousand-hooded serpent Shesha (Ananta) upon the ocean of milk.

questions.q: What is the name of the discus that Vishnu wields as a weapon?
questions.explanation: Vishnu bears the spinning Sudarshana chakra, his foremost weapon for upholding dharma.

questions.q: How many arms is Vishnu most commonly depicted with in iconography?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is most often depicted four-armed, bearing the conch, discus, mace, and lotus.

questions.q: What is the name of the conch shell that Vishnu holds?
questions.explanation: Vishnu blows the conch Panchajanya, later borne by his avatar Krishna.

questions.q: On which cosmic ocean is Vishnu traditionally described as reclining?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines on the Kshira Sagara, the cosmic ocean of milk, in his abode of Vaikuntha.

questions.q: Why does Vishnu descend into the world as an avatar, according to tradition?
options: To restore dharma when it declines
questions.explanation: Vishnu descends as avatars whenever righteousness (dharma) declines, to restore cosmic order.

questions.q: What flower does Vishnu commonly hold in one of his four hands?
questions.explanation: Among his four emblems, Vishnu holds a lotus, symbol of purity and cosmic unfolding.

questions.q: What is the name of the mace (gada) carried by Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Vishnu wields the mace Kaumodaki as one of his four principal attributes.

questions.q: In how many primary avatars (the Dashavatara) is Vishnu most traditionally said to descend?
questions.explanation: The Dashavatara names ten principal avatars of Vishnu, from Matsya to Kalki.

questions.q: Which is the first avatar of Vishnu in the usual Dashavatara list?
questions.explanation: Matsya the fish is reckoned the first avatar, who saved the sage Manu from the great deluge.

questions.q: As which avatar does Vishnu support Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean?
questions.explanation: As Kurma the tortoise, Vishnu bore Mount Mandara on his back during the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu raised the earth from the cosmic waters on his tusks?
questions.explanation: As Varaha the boar, Vishnu lifted the earth (Bhudevi) from the depths on his tusks.

questions.q: As which avatar did Vishnu slay the demon Hiranyakashipu to protect the devotee Prahlada?
questions.explanation: As Narasimha, half-man and half-lion, Vishnu slew Hiranyakashipu to save his devotee Prahlada.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is the hero of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama, prince of Ayodhya and slayer of Ravana, is the avatar of Vishnu central to the Ramayana.

questions.q: As the dwarf avatar Vamana, what did Vishnu request from the demon king Bali?
questions.explanation: As Vamana, Vishnu asked Bali for three paces of land, then strode across all the worlds.

questions.q: What is the celestial abode of Vishnu called in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Vaikuntha is the supreme abode of Vishnu, where he dwells with Lakshmi.

questions.q: From which part of Vishnu is the god Brahma said to emerge in Puranic accounts?
options: A lotus rising from his navel
questions.explanation: In the Puranas, Brahma is born from a lotus that springs from the navel of the reclining Vishnu.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is counted as the eighth in the Dashavatara and spoke the Bhagavad Gita?
questions.explanation: Krishna, the eighth avatar, delivered the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the field of Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which avatar is foretold as the final, future descent of Vishnu at the end of the present age?
questions.explanation: Kalki, the prophesied tenth avatar, is said to appear at the close of the Kali Yuga to restore dharma.

questions.q: Which sacred gem or jewel is said to adorn the chest of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha jewel, said to have arisen from the churning of the ocean, adorns the chest of Vishnu.

questions.q: What is the name of the curl of hair or auspicious mark on Vishnu's chest?
questions.explanation: The Srivatsa, an auspicious mark or curl of hair, is described upon the chest of Vishnu.

questions.q: What is the name of Vishnu's sword in his classical iconography?
questions.explanation: Nandaka is the name of the sword attributed to Vishnu in traditional descriptions.

questions.q: In the Puranas, what is the name of Vishnu's bow?
questions.explanation: Sharanga is the celebrated bow of Vishnu, while Pinaka belongs to Shiva.

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean of milk, as which enchantress did Vishnu appear to distribute the nectar of immortality?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the form of the enchantress Mohini to recover the amrita from the asuras.

questions.q: Which warrior-sage avatar of Vishnu wielded an axe and is said to have rid the earth of tyrant kshatriyas?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, the axe-bearing Brahmin avatar of Vishnu, is said to have humbled the warrior kings.

questions.q: The name 'Vishnu' is traditionally derived from a Sanskrit root carrying which meaning?
questions.explanation: The name Vishnu is traditionally derived from the root 'vish,' meaning 'to pervade,' marking him as the all-pervading.

questions.q: Which devotional hymn of a thousand names of Vishnu appears within the Mahabharata?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu Sahasranama, the thousand names of Vishnu, is recited by Bhishma in the Mahabharata.
